weather_line: Clearing by mid-morning · fog holding in the eastern hollows · optimistic for the weekend

stories.headline: Steve Names the Fold: What Lies Forty Miles East of Jellico, and Why the Jeep Has Been Running Its Engine Every Morning This Week
stories.byline: By our Adventure Correspondent, filed before the dew lifted
body: Sources close to the household confirmed Friday morning that Steve has spoken the name aloud — not loudly, not ceremonially, but in the way he names things that matter: once, to Christine, over the map, with one finger on the fold. The location, forty miles east of Jellico in a crease of highland that Ministry cartographers have designated 'administratively complex' for eleven consecutive years, is called Heron Shelf. It sits at the junction of two ridgelines, accessible by a single unmarked track that Steve located via what he described as 'a hunch and a logging road that probably shouldn't still be there.'

What made it worth the second bookmark — and, it can now be reported, a third small pencil dot in the margin that Steve has not yet acknowledged — is a rock formation at the shelf's eastern edge that appears, from the only available photograph (taken by a lost orienteer in 2003 and filed incorrectly under 'miscellaneous atmospheric phenomena'), to overhang a hollow large enough to stand in. Christine studied the photograph for ninety seconds. She then asked Steve what the track's gradient was in the final quarter-mile. He told her. She nodded once. The Adventure Desk is treating this as a departure announcement.
The Jeep has been running its engine each morning for five minutes. Steve says this is 'just keeping things warm.' The Jeep has not commented, but its tyre pressure is, by all accounts, immaculate.

stories.headline: Ragnok Wins Re-election by Seventeen Votes; Credits 'Sound Counsel, Excellent Recordkeeping, and a Reference I Intend to Have Framed'
body: The Gringotts Election Board announced Thursday evening that Ragnok has been returned as Chief Account-Keeper with a majority of seventeen votes — the largest margin in a contested election since 1887, when his predecessor won by sixteen and immediately disputed the tally. Griphook the Younger conceded in a statement described by observers as 'technically gracious and emotionally complicated.' He has since requested a procedural review of the vote-counting methodology, which uses an enchanted abacus and has been declared infallible by the same board that declared his triplicate rule invalid.

Ragnok accepted the result in a brief address to the vault floors. He thanked the electorate, acknowledged the board, and referred to Christine's character reference as 'the most precise seven sentences ever entered into goblin electoral record.' He has, as promised, had it framed. It currently hangs slightly crooked in his office. He has not straightened it, on the grounds that it looked exactly right when he hung it and therefore the wall is wrong.

stories.headline: Glowing Briefcase Reclassified from 'Not a Concern' to 'a Matter Under Active Review'; Ministry Declines to Specify What Changed Between Tuesday and Now
body: The Ministry of Magic issued a revised statement Friday regarding the luminous briefcase observed in Level Three Corridor on Thursday, upgrading its official classification from 'not a concern' to 'a matter under active review' without explaining what prompted the change. A spokesperson noted that the case had been 'relocated for assessment purposes' and that its current position within the Ministry was 'known to the relevant parties.' When asked who the relevant parties were, the spokesperson indicated that this too was under review.

The briefcase was last seen near the lifts at half past four, glowing in the amber register that Ministry containment staff privately refer to as 'the interesting one.' It has not been opened. Several officials who passed it in the corridor reported feeling, inexplicably, that whatever was inside was exactly what they had been looking for without knowing they were looking. The Ministry has not addressed this element of the reports. The element of the reports has not gone away.

stories.headline: Household Enters Weekend in Strong Position: All Departments Report, Disputes Are Few, and a Certain Gentleman Has Selected His Lap in Advance
body: Friday evening conditions across the household are described by bureau correspondents as 'stable with a high ceiling.' The kitchen is tidy. The map is flat. The Jeep knows what it is preparing for. Three familiars are distributed across their customary stations with the precise territorial confidence of a household that has sorted itself out over many Fridays and sees no reason to renegotiate now.

Jersey submitted his weekend positioning request at approximately three in the afternoon and has already secured the warmest available cushion, which he is treating as both residence and personal statement. Lucy has been quiet all day, which the Bureau notes is itself a kind of news. Ellie spent twenty minutes this morning in thoughtful conversation with the glass door, parted from it warmly, and has since moved on. The weekend, by all accounts, is ready.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Quieter than usual on Friday, which is not evidence of anything, and she would like that noted clearly and entered into the record.
Ellie: Made a new friend near the back door this morning — very friendly, very still, turned out to be the boot scraper — and has chosen to remember the encounter fondly regardless.
Jersey: Has pre-selected his weekend lap, lodged a formal preference for the left cushion, and is now simply waiting for the weekend to confirm what he has already decided.
founding_editor_note: That rock formation in the photograph? Cantilever. Whoever built it knew what they were doing. I'm not saying I could have done it better, but I'm not saying I couldn't. — D.

in_brief.sport: Friday Strength Report: all loads managed, all loads accounted for, the house stands.
in_brief.forecast: Fog in the eastern hollows through Saturday morning, lifting by ten — ideal conditions for anyone who happens to know where an unmarked logging road is.

storyline_updates.title: The Second Bookmark
storyline_updates.summary: Steve has named the location aloud: Heron Shelf, forty miles east of Jellico, a rock formation overhanging a hollow large enough to stand in, accessed by a track that probably shouldn't still exist. Christine studied the only photograph for ninety seconds and asked about the gradient. He answered. The Adventure Desk considers departure imminent. Day 4 should cover the departure itself — or the morning of it — and what the shelf turns out to be when they arrive.

storyline_updates.title: The Glowing Briefcase
storyline_updates.summary: A new arc opens: the Ministry's luminous briefcase, previously classified 'not a concern,' has been reclassified 'under active review' with no explanation for what changed. It was seen near the Level Three lifts at half past four, glowing amber. Everyone who passed it felt it contained exactly what they'd been looking for. It has not been opened. Day 2 should deepen the mystery — perhaps it moves again, or someone attempts to open it, or its origins begin to surface.
